rules.rule: Provide context about the frontend project structure and technologies.

context.description: This frontend project is built with Next.js (App Router), React, and TypeScript. It uses Tailwind CSS for styling and Jest for testing. It communicates with a backend API (configured via environment variables) and handles authentication.

directoryStructure.src/app/: Next.js App Router pages, layouts, loading states, and API routes
directoryStructure.src/lib/: Utility functions, API client (api.ts), auth helpers (auth.ts), env handling (env.ts)
directoryStructure.src/types/: TypeScript interfaces and type definitions (if present)
directoryStructure.public/: Static assets (images, fonts, scripts like env.js)
directoryStructure..env or .env.local: Environment variables (API URL, Socket URL, etc.)
directoryStructure.Dockerfile: Instructions for building the Docker image

conventions: Follow Next.js App Router conventions for file-based routing and components (page.tsx, layout.tsx, loading.tsx, error.tsx).
Use server components and client components ('use client') appropriately.
Styling is primarily done using Tailwind CSS utility classes.
Adhere to TypeScript best practices and defined types.
